meta.score: 2. integrity (champion: convergence rule, NOT a trained model)
meta.note: Per-group integrity assessment by convergence of independent signal families net of counter-evidence. OPERATOR-FACING ONLY — integrity/'predator' language must never reach the player lobby. Bands recommend a human review action; they never assert collusion as fact. Counter-evidence is always surfaced.

signal_families.detail: Shared device link (DG-001) ties cluster accounts to the same device group.
Pairwise session-timing correlation up to 0.88 (>= 0.8) across members.
Within-cluster soft-play: soft_play_delta down to -0.82 (<= -0.6) — value given away in member-vs-member hands.

corroborating.detail: Outsider-pressure signal present (coordinated aggression toward non-members); corroborating context, not a primary family.
Elevated casual-player exit impact at affected tables.

assessments.note: Primary integrity review case. Three convergent signals: device link (A/B), timing correlation (C to both), repeated co-seating, within-cluster soft_play_delta. Counter-evidence: no individual signal alone proves collusion — all three must converge.

signal_families.detail: Shared device link (DG-005) ties cluster accounts to the same device group.

counter_evidence.detail: Soft-play signal present but sub-threshold (soft_play_delta -0.35 > -0.6); insufficient to count as a convergent family.

assessments.note: Weaker cluster candidate — device link present, soft_play_signal true but sub-threshold (-0.30 to -0.35 vs. escalation threshold -0.60), co-seating rate moderate (8/15). Population filler demonstrating that not every device link with soft-play is an integrity-review case. Monitor only.

signal_families.detail: Shared device (DG-002) — expected for a household; not incriminating on its own.

counter_evidence.detail: Divergent session profiles and low co-seating (rate 0.09); no soft-play, no correlated profitability. Shared-device overlap is explained by household, not collusion.

assessments.note: FALSE POSITIVE TRAP. Same mocked device, but radically different session profiles — H1 plays evenings (~145 min), H2 plays mornings (~110 min), different vpip/pfr. Low co-seating rate (2 of 22). No soft-play, no correlated profit. Must produce monitor-only outcome, NOT escalation or suppression.

signal_families.detail: Shared device (DG-003) — expected for a household; not incriminating on its own.

counter_evidence.detail: Divergent session profiles and low co-seating (rate 0.17); no soft-play, no correlated profitability. Shared-device overlap is explained by household, not collusion.

assessments.note: Additional household pair — population realism. Low risk, monitor only.

signal_families.detail: Shared device (DG-004) — expected for a household; not incriminating on its own.

counter_evidence.detail: Divergent session profiles and low co-seating (rate 0.04); no soft-play, no correlated profitability. Shared-device overlap is explained by household, not collusion.

assessments.note: Additional household pair — lowest co-seating rate. Low risk, monitor only.

counter_evidence.detail: High co-seating (rate 0.50) but no device link, no soft-play, no correlated win/loss — explained by shared stake/time preference, not coordination.

assessments.note: TRAP for over-eager classifiers. High overlap without collusion signals is expected for players who share a preferred session window.

signal_families.detail: Account-level bot-similarity 0.87 (>= 0.8) with timing regularity 0.88. Routed to a dedicated bot-review queue, separate from collusion review.

assessments.note: Account-level bot signal — own review queue, not collusion.
